name: Sierra de Gata and the A Fala Villages Loop
name_original: Castillo de Trevejo y Acebo - Sierra de Gata — circular por San Martín de Trevejo
description: This difficult 48.1-mile road cycling route, Castillo de Trevejo y Acebo, offers challenging climbs and scenic views in Sierra de Gata.
region_intro: Road cycling around Villasrubias offers routes through the Sierra de Gata mountain range, characterized by rolling hills and forested areas. The region features a network of roads connecting small villages, providing varied terrain for cyclists. Elevations can be significant, with some routes including climbs over 1,000 meters. The landscape transitions between open agricultural land and denser woodlands, offering diverse scenic backdrops. Best road cycling routes around Villasrubias The most popular road cycling route is Sierra de Gata and the A Fala Villages Loop, a difficult 48.1 miles (77.5 km) trail that takes 4 hours 2 minutes to complete. This route explores the Sierra de Gata region, connecting several villages. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Border with Portugal - from Aldeia da Ponte to Fuenteguinaldo — circular through the lands of Rebollar, a moderate 39.3 miles (63.3 km) path. This route follows the border region with Portugal, offering views of the Rebollar lands. Local road cyclists also love the Viewpoint of Hoyos – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Barrio El Camping-La Rivera, a difficult 54.9 miles (88.4 km) trail leading through the local countryside, often completed in about 4 hours 43 minutes. Road cycling around Villasrubias is defined by rolling hills, forested sections, and routes connecting traditional villages. The network offers options for both moderate and difficult ability levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Villasrubias's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What is the general difficulty level of road cycling routes around Villasrubias?
A: Road cycling routes in the Villasrubias region are generally categorized as moderate to difficult. There are 11 moderate routes and 8 difficult routes available, with no easy options, indicating a challenging terrain suitable for experienced cyclists.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on road cycling routes in Villasrubias?
A: The terrain around Villasrubias is characterized by rolling hills and forested sections within the Sierra de Gata mountain range. Routes often connect small villages, offering varied landscapes that transition between open agricultural land and denser woodlands. Expect significant elevations, with some climbs exceeding 1,000 meters.

Q: Are there any circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, many routes around Villasrubias are circular. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025845812/sierra-de-gata-and-the-a-fala-villages-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Gata and the A Fala Villages Loop&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 48.1-mile (77.5 km) circular route, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1025861383/border-with-portugal-from-aldeia-da-ponte-to-fuenteguinaldo-circular-through-the-lands-of-rebollar" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Border with Portugal - from Aldeia da Ponte to Fuenteguinaldo — circular through the lands of Rebollar&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 39.3-mile (63.3 km) loop.

Q: What are the typical distances for road cycling routes in this area?
A: Routes in Villasrubias vary in distance, generally ranging from around 39 miles (63 km) to over 55 miles (88 km). For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/1275619" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Fuenteguinaldo – Navasfrías Town Center loop from Fuenteguinaldo&lt;/a&gt; is 52.5 miles (84.4 km), while the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20133207" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledillo de Gata – Cathedral of Santa María loop from Descargamaría&lt;/a&gt; covers 58.4 miles (93.9 km).

Q: Are there any scenic viewpoints along the road cycling routes?
A: Yes, the region offers several scenic spots. You can find stunning views at the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6968398" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorro de los Ángeles Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt;, which is accessible from some routes. The general landscape of the Sierra de Gata also provides numerous panoramic vistas.

Q: Can I find places to eat or drink along the routes?
A: Given that many routes connect small villages, you will typically find cafes, bars, and restaurants in these settlements. Planning your route to pass through villages like Robledillo de Gata or Torre de Don Miguel will provide opportunities for refreshments.

Q: Are there any natural pools or wild swimming spots near the road cycling trails?
A: Yes, the area is known for its natural pools. You can find the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4840158" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Natural Pool&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053456" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Natural Pool of Descargamaría&lt;/a&gt;, which are popular spots for cooling off and enjoying the natural surroundings.

Q: What historical or cultural attractions can I explore while road cycling in Villasrubias?
A: The region is dotted with charming villages and historical sites. You can visit the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4821468" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Almenara&lt;/a&gt;, a historic tower, or explore the picturesque settlements of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6054062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Robledillo de Gata&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5690787" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torre de Don Miguel&lt;/a&gt;, which offer a glimpse into local culture and architecture.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Villasrubias?
A: The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant conditions for road cycling in Villasrubias, with milder temperatures. Summers can be quite hot, especially in the valleys, while winters in the Sierra de Gata can bring colder temperatures and potentially some precipitation at higher elevations.

Q: Is road cycling in Villasrubias suitable for families with children?
A: Given that all routes are rated as moderate to difficult, with significant elevation gains and distances, the road cycling in Villasrubias is generally not recommended for families with young children or beginner cyclists. The terrain is more suited for experienced riders seeking a challenge.

Q: Where can I park my car when starting a road cycling route?
A: While specific trailhead parking information isn't always detailed, most routes begin and end in or near the small villages that characterize the region. You can typically find public parking options within these villages, such as Villasrubias itself, Fuenteguinaldo, or Descargamaría, which serve as convenient starting points.

Q: Are there any waterfalls to see along the routes?
A: Yes, the region features beautiful natural attractions, including the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6053913" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrituelo Waterfall&lt;/a&gt;. While not directly on every road cycling route, it's a notable natural monument in the vicinity that can be accessed or viewed as part of a cycling excursion.
tips: Viewpoint from where you can see the views of the town of San Martín de Trevejo and in addition to the mountain range that is located in front called Sierra de Cachaza. Nice valley that descends from Sierra de Gata.

To the north of Cáceres, more specifically in Sierra de Gata, is the Castle of Trevejo. This castle is famous for its history and for its typical architecture and its impressive location. There are incredible views of the town and the Sierra de Gata complex.

Viewpoint that is located near the town of Los Hoyos and from where you can see the extension of the nucleus and the valley where it is located. The Sierra de Santa Olalla descends until reaching Los Hoyos.
Charming place. First for the environment and then for the facilities. Natural pools with cool water on the hottest days of the Extremaduran summer. Sunny and shady areas!

faqs.question: Is this route truly suitable for road cycling, given its name and the region?
faqs.answer: While this route is categorized as 'racebike' on komoot, it's important to note that the Sierra de Gata region, including areas around San Martín de Trevejo and Acebo, often features varied terrain. Local research suggests that many routes in this area, even those with 'circular' descriptions, can include unpaved sections or 'pista de tierra' (dirt tracks). Therefore, while parts may be suitable for road bikes, be prepared for potentially challenging sections that might be better suited for mountain bikes or gravel bikes. Always check recent conditions if possible.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to cycle this route?
faqs.answer: The best times to cycle this route are typically spring (April-May) and autumn (September-October). During these seasons, the weather is generally mild and pleasant, avoiding the intense heat of summer and the colder, potentially wetter conditions of winter. This allows for more comfortable cycling and better enjoyment of the scenic landscapes.

faqs.question: What are the main points of interest along the route?
faqs.answer: This circular route offers several notable points of interest. You'll have the opportunity to see the historic Castillo De Trevejo, enjoy panoramic views from the Miraol dus Arrevises and the Viewpoint of Hoyos, and pass by the Natural pools of the Sierra de Gata.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the route, particularly near San Martín de Trevejo?
faqs.answer: San Martín de Trevejo is a common starting point for routes in the area. While specific parking lots for this exact route aren't detailed, you can typically find public parking within or on the outskirts of San Martín de Trevejo village. It's advisable to arrive early, especially during peak seasons, to secure a spot.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required to cycle in the ZEC Sierra de Gata?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for cycling on public trails within the ZEC Sierra de Gata. However, as it is a protected natural area, it's crucial to respect all environmental regulations, stay on marked paths, and avoid disturbing wildlife or vegetation. Always check for any local advisories before your visit.

faqs.question: Is the route dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While dogs are generally permitted in the Sierra de Gata natural area, it is highly recommended to keep them on a leash throughout the route. This is for their safety, the safety of other users, and to protect the local wildlife, especially as the route passes through a Special Area of Conservation (ZEC).

faqs.question: What is the terrain like on this circular route?
faqs.answer: The terrain on this route is varied and can be challenging. While some sections may be paved, expect to encounter unpaved roads, dirt tracks ('pista de tierra'), and potentially rougher surfaces, especially if the route incorporates older paths or rural roads. The Sierra de Gata is known for its diverse landscapes, including dense oak and chestnut groves, which contribute to the varied ground conditions.

faqs.question: Does this route intersect with any other official trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, this route incorporates sections of several official trails. You will find yourself on parts of the Cañada Real de Gata, Cañada Real, Cordel de Merinas, and the Camino Natural del Rio Ribera de Acebo, among others. These intersections can offer opportunities to explore shorter segments or connect to other routes if desired.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to complete this circular route?
faqs.answer: This route is approximately 77.5 km long with over 1360 meters of elevation gain, making it a difficult ride. On average, it takes about 4 hours to complete, but this can vary significantly based on your fitness level, the type of bike you're using (especially given the varied terrain), and how often you stop to enjoy the views or points of interest.

faqs.question: Are there places to find food, drinks, or accommodation along the route or nearby?
faqs.answer: Yes, the villages of San Martín de Trevejo, Acebo, and Trevejo, which are either on or very close to the route, offer amenities such as cafes, pubs, and accommodation options. These can be good spots to refuel, rest, or even stay overnight if you plan to extend your cycling adventure in the Sierra de Gata region.

waypoints.name: Natural pools of the Sierra de Gata
